leagueBriefing: Ludvig Åberg navigated a volatile Moving Day at TPC Sawgrass to maintain his grip on the lead, setting up a showdown between two of golf's brightest young stars in the final round of THE PLAYERS.

games.headline: Åberg maintains steady hand at Sawgrass to hold three-shot lead entering Sunday
games.brief: Ludvig Åberg survived a rollercoaster finish at TPC Sawgrass to card a 1-under 71, keeping him three strokes clear of the field heading into the final round. Despite a frustrating three-putt bogey on the 18th, Åberg’s efficiency—highlighted by a massive eagle on the 11th—offset the late stumbles. He now faces a final-round duel with fellow PGA TOUR University alum Michael Thorbjornsen, as both local residents look to claim the tour's flagship event on a course that punished nearly everyone else in the chasing pack.
bullets: Ludvig Åberg reached 13-under par overall, powered by his third eagle of the tournament on the par-5 11th hole.
Michael Thorbjornsen surged into the final pairing with a 5-under 67, the second-best round of the day.
Cameron Young sat just two back until a double-bogey on the 18th dropped him to 9-under, four shots off the lead.
Robert MacIntyre recorded the low round of Saturday with a 7-under 65 to climb into a tie for 10th.
A star-studded group at 8-under includes Justin Thomas, Viktor Hovland, and Matt Fitzpatrick, all five shots back.
games.context: Åberg is looking to become the first player since Craig Perks in 2002 to win THE PLAYERS Championship in his debut appearance.
